article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to close noise bylaw gaps for multi-unit residences
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report from Aria Rasouli that identifies gaps in how the City enforces its noise bylaw and how RCMP dispatch handles noise complaints in multi-unit buildings like apartments and condos. The report notes that municipal bylaws already apply to strata properties, but the City is looking at whether enforcement needs to be improved. A decision has not been made yet — this is an upcoming discussion item on the Council agenda.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and consider submitting your comments on noise enforcement in multi-unit buildings.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.1
Aria Rasouli - Gaps in Noise Bylaw Enforcement and RCMP Dispatch for Multi-Unit Residences
1.
Aria Rasouli - Gaps in Noise Bylaw Enforcement and RCMP Dispatch for Multi-Unit Residences.pdf
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Community Safety
Note from General Manager Community Safety
: Municipal bylaws apply throughout Burnaby, including strata properties, and the City retains authority to investigate potential bylaw contraventions where appropriate.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to review LED screen compliance at Gilmore Place
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to consider a resident's concerns about the LED screen at Gilmore Place, including whether it complies with the site's approved Master Sign Plan and Comprehensive Sign Plan. The resident also raised issues about litter and debris from nearby construction. Planning staff have reviewed the complaint and say the sign was approved through those plans. Council will decide if any further action is needed.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Protected bike lane on Winston Street in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will consider a proposal to build a protected bike lane on Winston Street between Sperling Avenue and Cariboo Road. This section of road is part of the Central Valley Greenway and is identified in the city's Priority Cycle Network. If approved, the new bike lane would change the street layout and could affect traffic flow, parking, and access for nearby businesses.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect street access and parking for your business.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.3
Protected Bike Lane on Winston Street
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Parks, Recreation and Culture, General Manager Engineering
Note from General Manager Engineering
: Winston Street between Sperling Avenue and Cariboo Road forms part of the Central Valley Greenway and is identified within the City's Priority Cycle Network in the Burnaby Transportation Plan.
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to study financial impact of new rental affordability rules
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider a financial feasibility analysis that will look at ways to make rental units more affordable in new buildings. The goal is to find a balance between keeping rents low for tenants and making sure developers can still build profitably. This analysis follows direction from Council on May 12, 2026. A final decision has not been made yet — this is a study phase to inform future policy.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the results of this analysis — it could lead to new affordability requirements for rental developments in Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
1.4
Reinhard Schauer - From Inclusion to Exclusion: Burnaby's Policy Pivot on Inclusionary Housing
1.
Reinhard Schauer - From Inclusion to Exclusion Burnaby's Policy Pivot on Inclusionary Housing.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 - Letter to Mayor and Council.pdf
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Planning and Development
Note from General Manager Planning and Development
: Staff are undertaking a financial feasibility analysis to determine potential measures to maximize affordability in inclusionary rental units while maintaining financial viability as per Council's direction from May 12, 2026.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Council to consider private water pipe repair rules after Hazel Street leak
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a report about a burst water pipe at 4689 Hazel Street. The leak was on a privately owned pipe, not a city watermain. The strata estimates $300,000 in replacement and elevator inspection costs. The resident requests the city require builders to cover repair costs and mandate damage insurance for utilities within 50 meters of construction sites. The decision has not been made yet.

Note from General Manager Engineering

City crews were contacted by the strata on June 1 at 9:42 PM to investigate a water leakage on their property.  It was discovered that the leak was from their privately owned water pipe and not a City watermain.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Metro Vancouver solid waste management plan update
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is considering a proposed update to Metro Vancouver's solid waste management plan. The draft plan includes a vision, guiding principles, goals, waste hierarchy, strategies, and actions for residual waste, regulatory options, and recycling centres. Businesses may need to adjust waste sorting and disposal practices if approved. Written feedback on the draft can be submitted to zerowaste@metrovancouver.org by August 1, 2026.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit written feedback on the draft plan to zerowaste@metrovancouver.org by August 1, 2026.

key_dates.label: Metro Vancouver solid waste plan comment period closes

agenda_briefs.industries: Food Service, Grocery & Food Retail, Construction & Development

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider aircraft noise over North Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to consider correspondence about aircraft noise over North Burnaby. This item is on the agenda for an upcoming meeting. The proposal would address noise concerns from planes flying over the area. A decision has not been made yet — this is a proposed discussion item that could lead to future action, such as a bylaw or a formal request to the federal government or NAV CANADA to change flight paths or noise levels.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if passed, it could affect noise levels for businesses and residents in North Burnaby.
agenda_briefs.industries: Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to consider Stage 3 watering restrictions
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposal to move the city to Stage 3 watering restrictions. This would mean a near-total ban on outdoor watering for most residents and businesses. The proposal is based on a letter from Darcy L. Olson. A final decision has not been made yet.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — Stage 3 restrictions would ban nearly all outdoor watering.
agenda_briefs.industries: Landlord & Property Mgmt, Agriculture, Construction & Development

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to consider Michael J. Fox Theatre closure
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to close the Michael J. Fox Theatre. The agenda item includes correspondence about the closure. A decision has not been made yet. If approved, this would affect community events, performances, and rentals at the theatre. Business owners who use the theatre for events or rely on its audiences should watch for the council decision.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on the theatre closure — this could affect your event bookings or customer traffic.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
3.4
Michael J. Fox Theatre Closure
Copy: Chief Administrative Officer, General Manager Corporate Services, General Manager Parks, Recreation and Culture
agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on fossil fuel correspondence action
agenda_briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal from a resident, Michael Lawrence, titled 'Take Action on Fossil Fuels'. The proposal asks Council to pass a resolution endorsing a Fossil Fuel Non-Proliferation Treaty and pledging to stop the expansion of fossil fuels in the community. The proposal has been sent to the Chief Administrative Officer and other senior city staff for review. A decision has not been made yet. If approved, this could lead to new city policies or bylaws that affect how Burnaby businesses use or rely on fossil fuels. Business owners should watch for the council decision to understand any potential impacts on their operations.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on this proposal — it could affect your business if new fossil fuel rules are adopted.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Council Correspondence and Public Notice Submissions Agenda — June 23, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on Silver Avenue rezoning
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will vote on first and second reading of Rezoning Bylaw No. 14832 for 6470 and 6508 Silver Avenue on June 23, 2026 at 5 pm. The proposal would rezone from RM3 to a Comprehensive Development District to permit two high-rise apartment buildings (32 and 28 storeys) with approximately 784 units, including 83 rental replacement units. No public hearing will be held; written comments must be submitted by 12:00 noon on June 16, 2026 to be included in the council package.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit written comments referencing REZ #25-12 by 12:00 noon on June 16, 2026 via email, webform, or mail to Legislative Services.

key_dates.label: Written comment deadline — Burnaby Silver Avenue rezoning REZ #25-12
Council vote on first and second reading — Burnaby Silver Avenue rezoning

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Rezoning of five properties on Dow Avenue in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will vote on First and Second Reading of Rezoning Bylaw No. 14833 (REZ #25-23) on June 23, 2026 at 5 pm. The bylaw would rezone 6645–6707 Dow Avenue from RM3 to a Comprehensive Development District to permit two high-rise apartment buildings (45 and 35 storeys) with commercial fronting Beresford Street, plus a 6-storey apartment building. Approximately 1,065 dwelling units are proposed, including 112 required rental replacement units. No public hearing will be held; written submissions are accepted but delegation requests will not be allowed.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Submit written comments referencing REZ #25-23 by 12:00 noon on June 16, 2026 to be included in the Council package.

key_dates.label: Written submission deadline for REZ #25-23 Council package
Council vote on First and Second Reading of Rezoning Bylaw No. 14833
